The '''Battle of Azure Spaceport''' was a conflict that occurred in the early stages of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in which [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] forces tracked the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] by means of a tracer device planted in a foolproof codebreaker. This had been brought by [[Talesan Fry]], who agreed to turn the codebreaker over to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] at a [[Azure Spaceport|hidden base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Azure/Legends|Azure]].

It possessed meager defenses and was severely outnumbered by the huge Separatist fleet. The Republic forces had to surrender control of the planet. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Solomahal/Legends|Solomahal]], the [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] of the base, was determined not to let the codebreaker into Separatist hands, thus facilitating its destruction prior to his capture.

==The Battle==
As the Republic forces discovered the Separatists were planning to launch their attack, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] took to their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] in an attempt to hold off the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] until [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone reinforcements]] could arrive. Padmé was sent with Taly in a starship away from the battle— intent on jumping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in time to avoid the conflict. Meanwhile, Anakin led the most skilled of the General Solomahal's [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] into battle against the massive force of [[Droid tri-fighter/Legends|droid tri-fighters]]. When the enemy began to fire on civilian ships, Padmé's was peppered with fire, and crashed, though two [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] were launched. Siri and Kenobi peeled off from the main fight and worked their way to where the pods' had landed.

As the Jedi realized that the battle would be lost due to the droids' superior numbers, they contacted Solomahal. They told him to send the codebreaker away, and to prepare for evacuation. Refusing the suggestion, the General continued the fight, guarding the codebreaker himself. The Jedi arrived at the landing platform, but were soon surprised by [[Magus (bounty hunter)|Magus]] in his [[Color/Legends|red]]-black starfighter, who immediately opened fire. Fleeing from his attack, they raced aboard [[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter/Legends|ARC-170 starfighters]], Padmé with Siri, and Taly with Obi-Wan.

On the other side of the battle, Anakin began to realize that though their casualties were low and the they had been doing well, they could not prevail against such a large army. After receiving a comm that Padmé's ship went down, he raced to the platform, hoping to save [[Gender/Legends|her]] if she had survived. As the Jedi and others fought against Magus, Siri concocted a risky plan. Crawling out of her ship and leaving Padmé in control, she leaped into the air and used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to land on Magus' ship, where she used her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to cut through the canopy. Magus began to fly wildly, jerking around, which forced Kenobi to launch a low-powered [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] in an attempt to stop him. The missile collided with the ship, knocking Siri into the cockpit and crashing the starfighter to the ground.

Magus was ejected from the ship, defeated. Obi-Wan jumped into the smoking ship to find Siri. He discovered that she had been shot by Magus, and barely alive. With her last breath, she gave Obi-Wan her warming crystal and confessed her love for him once more. Obi-Wan then began to allow his anger to overcome him, and came close to murdering Magus, but reigned in his emotions. He arrested Magus as a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] of war.

==Aftermath==
The droids bombed Taly's office and labs, destroying all of his inventions and work, most of which would be impossible to reproduce. Still fighting for a losing side, Solomahal was captured, though he destroyed the codebreaker shortly before his capture, stopping any chance of the Separatists obtaining it and its power. He later broke free and was given another command. Back at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Obi-Wan mourned Siri and walked through the halls, unable to sleep just like his master twenty years before when [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] lost [[Tahl]]. But in his pain, he finally understood what Qui-Gon had tried to teach him so long ago-that he could survive with the loss of the person he loved most. Obi-Wan had finally learned how to love with an open heart.
